mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the economy is in a post-shock recovery phase.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero during post-shock recovery periods over a 24-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding trend levels reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds the 10-year trend rate.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the 2.5 percent annual threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >70% of months where inflation breaches the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a low federal funds rate environment supports wage-driven consumption without triggering immediate rate hikes, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and broader economic deceleration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative returns in late-cycle periods, with positive returns occurring in >65% of cases over a 5-year backtest.
